description: The controller can operate as a PCIe Root Complex and manage downstream endpoints. The driver selects RC mode, configures capabilities, and initiates link training. After link-up it performs enumeration via configuration transactions. Access to downstream configuration is done through dedicated windows once L0 is reached. Robust sequencing requires clocks and SerDes lock before enabling LTSSM.
dependencies: Module power/clock enabled; DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE=RC; SerDes configured and locked; CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN
Remarks: Ensure SerDes is configured and use conservative timeouts while waiting for L0 before enumeration.

description: The controller supports single-function PCIe Endpoint operation. The driver programs EP mode and configures BAR apertures and capabilities for host access. Inbound translation regions handle host-initiated transactions to local memory. Interrupts are signaled using MSI or legacy INTx depending on platform support. Configuration follows host enumeration policies.
evidence: DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE; BARn; IB translation; MSI capability
dependencies: Module power/clock; DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE=EP; BARs and IB translation configured
Remarks: Prefer MSI over INTx for performance and ensure BAR sizes align with application needs.

description: The controller negotiates PCIe Gen1 or Gen2 data rates during training. Software configures Port Logic Gen2 parameters as required for Gen2 operation. Stable SerDes PLL lock is mandatory prior to enabling the LTSSM. The negotiated rate is reflected in link status and impacts throughput and latency. Equalization and clocking must meet board design constraints.
dependencies: Stable reference clock; SerDes PLL lock; PL_GEN2 configured
Remarks: Start training at Gen1 for bring-up if needed, then retrain to Gen2 after validating signal integrity.

feature_name: x1/x2 link width with dynamic width conversion
description: The controller operates with one or two lanes and can down-train if fewer lanes are available. Width negotiation occurs automatically during link training. No software-controlled split of x2 into two independent x1 links is supported. Performance scales with active lanes but remains functional with reduced width. Software primarily monitors negotiated width for policy decisions.
dependencies: SerDes lanes wired; proper pinmux; link training enabled
Remarks: Design routing to support the intended width and verify negotiated width via link status registers.

description: Automatic lane reversal compensates for differential lane swaps on the PCB. The feature engages during link initialization without explicit software programming. Operation is transparent to higher layers once the link is trained. The driver only needs to enable link training and verify link health. No runtime controls are typically required.
dependencies: SerDes lanes connected; LTSSM enabled
Remarks: Validate during bring-up with lane-swap boards and rely on hardware negotiation for stability.

description: Receiver polarity inversion support tolerates differential pair flips. Configuration may be automatic or limited to SerDes setup steps. After link-up, data integrity indicates correct polarity handling. The driver should only adjust if diagnostics reveal polarity issues. Normal operation requires no periodic software intervention.
dependencies: SerDes configured; link training/diagnostics available
Remarks: Use link diagnostics and error counters to confirm polarity handling after initial training.

feature_name: Single Virtual Channel and Traffic Class
description: The controller implements a single Virtual Channel and one Traffic Class. This simplifies traffic management and eliminates the need for VC arbitration. Credit management is handled automatically by hardware. The driver aligns expectations and does not attempt to configure multiple VCs. QoS must be achieved using other available tagging mechanisms.
dependencies: Hardware fixed capability; standard PCIe config space
Remarks: Do not attempt multi-VC negotiation; design QoS using priority or ordering attributes instead.

description: Hardware limits outbound TLP payloads to 128 bytes and inbound to 256 bytes. The maximum read request size is 256 bytes. The driver programs Device Control to enforce MPS and MRRS accordingly. DMA and memcpy sizes should respect these limits to avoid segmentation. Exceeding limits reduces performance due to extra TLPs and completions.
evidence: PCIe Capability Device Control.MPS; Device Control.MRRS
dependencies: PCIe Capability Device Control; application limits documented
Remarks: Configure MPS/MRRS early and validate effective values by reading back Device Control after training.

description: The controller supports PCIe Advanced Error Reporting for detailed fault logging. Correctable and uncorrectable status registers allow fine-grained diagnosis. Optional ECRC generation/check can be enabled depending on system support. The driver enables reporting, masks as needed, and clears logs on service. Integration with system health monitoring is recommended for reliability.
dependencies: AER capability; PCIE_ACCR; PCIE_CERR/UNCERR and masks
Remarks: Capture first error pointers promptly in ISR paths and rate-limit noisy endpoints with policy controls.

description: Active State Power Management states L0s and L1 reduce link power at light loads. The driver coordinates entry and exit policies based on latency budgets. Negotiation occurs with the link partner through standard PCIe mechanisms. Excessive latency sensitivity may require disabling or biasing ASPM settings. L2 state is not supported and must not be requested.
dependencies: PCIe PM capability; PMCFG; platform policy
Remarks: Align ASPM settings with system sleep policies and verify wake latency under target workloads.

feature_name: Legacy INTx support (RC receive / EP generate)
description: Legacy INTx signaling is supported for compatibility with older systems. In RC mode, the controller receives and services INTx from endpoints. In EP mode, the device can assert INTx toward the host. The driver enables and clears legacy interrupt bits and routes events to the SoC interrupt controller. Prefer MSI when available to reduce latency and sharing issues.
dependencies: LEGACY_* IRQ regs; SoC interrupt routing; IRQ_EOI
Remarks: Use INTx only when MSI is unavailable or during early bring-up to simplify host configuration.

description: Message Signaled Interrupts provide efficient event delivery without sideband pins. Endpoint mode programs MSI address and data then triggers writes to signal events. Root Complex mode enables MSI reception and dispatches vectors to handlers. The driver prioritizes MSI over INTx where supported. Vector allocation and affinity should match workload distribution.
dependencies: MSI capability; MSI*_IRQ_ENABLE_SET/CLR; SoC IRQ routing
Remarks: Validate MSI address/data programming with test writes and confirm vector delivery in ISR logs.

description: PHY loopback enables link-path validation during bring-up. The driver enables loopback controls and monitors LTSSM and error counters. This aids isolation of board-level signal integrity issues. Loopback testing should be performed before device enumeration. Disable loopback before normal traffic to avoid unintended isolation.
dependencies: Port Logic/PHY loopback controls; DEBUG0/1
Remarks: Use loopback with short test windows and reset the link afterward to restore normal operation.

description: Outbound windows translate SoC addresses into PCIe memory, I/O, or config TLPs. The driver sizes windows with OB_SIZE and programs offsets and attributes. Once enabled, software can reach remote devices within programmed apertures. Careful sizing prevents unintended access to peer resources. Disable or tighten windows during error recovery.
dependencies: OB_SIZE; OB_OFFSET_INDEX/HI; CMD_STATUS.OB_XLT_EN
Remarks: Group outbound regions by usage (config vs memory) and document address maps for firmware users.

description: Inbound regions map PCIe addresses into SoC memory for host-initiated access. The driver matches BARs and programs start and offset registers per region. Enabling inbound translation allows safe reception of posted and non-posted requests. Aperture sizing is critical to protect unrelated memory. Review protection policies before exposing inbound paths.
dependencies: IB_BARn; IB_STARTn_LO/HI; IB_OFFSETn; CMD_STATUS.IB_XLT_EN
Remarks: Keep inbound regions minimal and revoke access rapidly during teardown or fault conditions.

description: In RC mode, dedicated windows allow software to access downstream configuration and I/O spaces. The driver selects bus, device, and function via CFG_SETUP. 32-bit single accesses are used per PCIe rules for config space. These windows are used during enumeration and device setup. Access must be serialized during reset and retraining events.
dependencies: CFG_SETUP; IOBASE; 32-bit non-burst access rules
Remarks: Guard config cycles with link-state checks and retry logic to handle hot-plug or retrain races.

description: BAR configuration defines the addressable regions exposed by the endpoint. The driver programs BAR sizes and types and optionally enables filtering to block invalid accesses. DBI CS2 may be needed to program BAR masks on some implementations. Correct BAR setup ensures host software maps resources as intended. Filtering reduces attack surface and prevents stray accesses.
dependencies: BAR registers; CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2; filtering controls
Remarks: After programming, read back BARs and masks to confirm size encoding and alignment requirements.

description: Completion timeout detection helps identify stalled or lost transactions. The driver configures timeout thresholds to balance sensitivity and noise. Transaction filtering can drop or block certain classes to aid recovery from misbehaving peers. Proper logging supports root cause analysis. Settings should be conservative initially and tightened after validation.
dependencies: Completion timeout controls; filtering registers
Remarks: Record offending requester IDs and throttle traffic while implementing staged recovery actions.

description: Endianness control ensures correct byte order between the big-endian CPU and PCIe devices. The driver programs the ENDIAN register for inbound and outbound paths as needed. Known data patterns should validate correctness before enabling DMA. Mismatched settings can silently corrupt data. Apply changes during quiescent periods to avoid partial transfers.
dependencies: ENDIAN register; big-endian swap tables
Remarks: Start with PIO tests before DMA and capture sanity patterns to build confidence in configuration.

description: Privilege and priority tags on master transactions inform access control and QoS. The driver sets MST_PRIV, PRIVID, and priority fields based on system policy. Proper tagging helps isolate untrusted traffic and prioritize latency-sensitive flows. Misconfiguration can lead to starvation or privilege escalation. Settings should be audited with system security requirements.
dependencies: PRIORITY register (MST_PRIV, PRIVID, MST_PRIORITY)
Remarks: Coordinate with system interconnect policy to ensure tags propagate correctly end-to-end.

description: Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop attributes can improve performance when ordering or cache coherence constraints allow. The driver controls these flags through TLPCFG fields. Use is workload-dependent and may be unsafe across certain bridges or non-coherent endpoints. Thorough testing is required before enabling globally. Defaults should favor strict ordering for correctness.
dependencies: TLPCFG.RELAXED; TLPCFG.NO_SNOOP
Remarks: Enable selectively for bulk DMA paths and leave control traffic as strictly ordered.

feature_name: LTSSM control and link status debug
description: Software controls link training by setting the LTSSM enable bit. The driver polls debug registers to observe state transitions and verify L0. Additional debug counters and flags assist with fault isolation. Clear, retry, and capture snapshots during failures for later analysis. Control is essential during bring-up and recovery sequences.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN; Port Logic DEBUG0/1
Remarks: Implement bounded polling with backoff and surface states to higher layers for diagnostics.

description: The controller can issue a downstream hot reset to recover from errors. The driver asserts the reset command and waits for link re-training. Proper quiesce of traffic prevents incomplete transfers. After reset, software revalidates capabilities and windows before resuming. Use sparingly to avoid unnecessary service disruption.
dependencies: RSTCMD.INIT_RST; FLUSH_N handling; LTSSM state
Remarks: Quiesce DMA and posted writes before reset, and re-enumerate only affected devices.

description: Status bits indicate whether inbound or outbound buffers contain data. The driver can poll these bits during diagnostics or quiesce sequences. In steady state they are rarely needed but help confirm idle conditions. Using them before resets avoids data loss. They are not a substitute for protocol-level flow control.
dependencies: ACT_STATUS.IB_NOT_EMPTY/OB_NOT_EMPTY
Remarks: Read immediately prior to resets and record transitions to correlate with error logs.

feature_name: No integrated DMA engine (use SoC EDMA)
description: The controller does not include a built-in DMA engine; data movement uses the SoC EDMA over the interconnect. The driver configures EDMA channels and aligns transactions with payload limits. Throughput depends on DMA setup and PCIe link parameters. CPU-driven copies are reserved for control paths and small transfers.
dependencies: EDMA available on SoC; VBUSM master/slave ports
Remarks: Leverage scatter-gather and ring-based EDMA setups to maximize sustained bandwidth.

description: Inbound I/O TLPs are not accepted by the controller. Software must avoid mapping inbound I/O spaces and use memory space instead. Root Complex mode may still issue outbound I/O where required. This constraint simplifies decoding and reduces risk. System software must honor this limitation during resource allocation.
dependencies: Design limitation per spec; IO windows outbound only
Remarks: Use MMIO for inbound access and validate that endpoints expose memory BARs for control/status.

feature_name: Single link; no switch or x2 split
description: The controller implements a single PCIe link and cannot act as a switch. A x2 link cannot be split into two independent x1 ports by software. Board-level fan-out requires an external switch device. Topology planning must account for the single-port nature. Lane bonding is handled internally only for the single link.
dependencies: Hardware topology constraint; SerDes lane bonding
Remarks: If fan-out is required, select a downstream PCIe switch and validate compatibility and power budget.

description: The controller supports device power management states excluding D3 cold with auxiliary power. The driver coordinates PM messages and manages context across L1 entries. Vaux-based retention should not be assumed. Resume paths must restore configuration and translation settings. Policies must respect platform power rails and wake constraints.
dependencies: PM capability; PMCMD/PMCFG; platform power rails
Remarks: Test suspend and resume under traffic and confirm MSI and windows are restored correctly.

description: SerDes PLL and related settings must be programmed prior to link training. The driver sequences writes with any required unlock steps on affected devices. Software polls lock status to ensure stability before enabling the LTSSM. Timing margins may need tuning per board. Failure to lock leads to unreliable training and errors.
dependencies: PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL; PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK; Kicker unlock (KeyStone I)
Remarks: Program SerDes early during boot and add retries with bounded delays for marginal conditions.

description: Error logging captures sources and supports masking to control reporting. The driver reads, records, and clears status in interrupt context. First error pointers enable focused triage. During recovery, traffic may be throttled to contain impact. Logs should be exported to system health monitors for correlation.
evidence: AER regs; PCIE_CERR/UNCERR and MASK regs
dependencies: AER regs; PCIE_CERR/UNCERR and MASK regs
Remarks: Snapshot logs before clearing and implement exponential backoff for repeated offenders.
